title: Is the wali (guardian) required for a valid nikah?
stanceSummary: Yasir Qadhi surveys classical and contemporary views: the majority (including Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i) hold the wali is required for the marriage contract; the Hanbali school has well-known positions that allow a mature woman more say when the wali unreasonably refuses a suitable match. He stresses balancing parental rights with spousal rights, avoiding unconditional parental obedience when it harms the spouse, and seeking qualified scholars for case-specific guidance.
alternateQuestions: Is wali mandatory for marriage in Islam?
Can a woman marry without her father's permission?
What if my wali refuses my choice of spouse?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hFAVGqxzx_E) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hFAVGqxzx_E&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi addresses this question.

- Scholars differ on whether **wali is a strict requirement** for every nikah, with detailed classical debate.
- Obedience to parents is **not unconditional** when it conflicts with the **rights of one’s spouse** or entails sin.
- Modern context (individualism vs. family) must be weighed **without discarding** the Shari'ah’s framework for family roles.

---
